summary: All steps complete. Replaced Pick<MessageContext,...> with standalone interface in bridge.ts, removing claudeStore import. Upgraded typecheck guard from bidirectional assignability to Equals<A,B> mapped-type assertion. Typecheck passes, 846/846 tests pass. Verified Equals catches field renames.

Verify current codebase compiles: run `npm run typecheck` in gui/ to establish green baseline before any changes; Update types.typecheck.ts: Replace weak bidirectional assignability assertions (_fwd/_rev) with an Equals<A,B> mapped-type utility that checks exact structural equality. This catches field renames, additions, and removals even when all fields are optional. Add a comment documenting that the all-optional weakness exists for assignability but is mitigated by the Equals check. The expected type remains `Pick<MessageContext, 'selectedEntity' | 'diagnostics' | 'constraints'>`. This should compile now (types are currently identical) and will continue to compile after the standalone revert.; Revert bridge.ts: (a) Remove `import type { MessageContext } from './stores/claudeStore'` from line 8. (b) Replace `export type ClaudeMessageContext = Pick<MessageContext, ...>` (line 115) with a standalone interface: `export interface ClaudeMessageContext { selectedEntity?: string; diagnostics?: string[]; constraints?: string[]; }`. (c) Add a JSDoc comment above the interface pointing to `__tests__/types.typecheck.ts` as the structural drift guard that keeps this in sync with MessageContext. The claudeSendMessage function signature and body remain unchanged.; Verify everything compiles and tests pass: run `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) to confirm the standalone interface stays in sync via the Equals assertion, then run `npm test` to confirm runtime behavior is unchanged (claudeBridge.test.ts, bridge.test.ts, etc.).

Fixed broken compile-time assertion: replaced no-op conditional-to-never pattern with AssertTrue<T extends true> constrained generic. Verified negative test: adding _drift field to _ExpectedClaudeContext correctly produces TS2344 error. All 846 tests pass, typecheck green.
